title: Treehouse Of Horror Xxi

jokes.text: Why, you little...
jokes.punchline: The familiar opening of his signature throttling
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfect character integration and economy, but not original anymore

jokes.text: Beneath this smile, I'm in awful, awful pain!
jokes.setup: Krusty's clown persona maintaining his fake smile
jokes.punchline: Revealing the existential despair beneath the entertainment
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty dark humor contrasting his public persona with private misery

jokes.text: Welcome, precious primetime viewers, valued Internet downloaders, and scary digital pirates!
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging illegal downloading with mock formality
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on viewing habits, though feels a bit forced

jokes.text: I went too far! Spoiled the whole show for everybody! I don't deserve to wield the remote. Oh, I'm getting old and gray... I'm dead.
jokes.setup: Krusty realizing he ruined the viewing experience
jokes.punchline: Escalating from guilt to melodramatic death
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but feels overwrought for the payoff

jokes.text: Monster go watch The Office.
jokes.setup: Cookie Monster appearing as horror host
jokes.punchline: Speaking in characteristic third person about mundane TV
jokes.explanation: Good character voice consistency, unexpected crossover reference

jokes.text: Dunder-Mifflin just like monster's workplace.
jokes.setup: Cookie Monster relating to The Office
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Sesame Street is like a corporate office
jokes.explanation: Clever connection between children's show and workplace comedy

jokes.text: Milhouse, do we have enough points for a weapons upgrade? Yeah! We really racked them up eating all those elf babies.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning eating elf babies for points
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about video game violence, good casual delivery

jokes.text: Ha-ha! Nothing beats a weapon made of weapons.
jokes.punchline: Illogical recursive weapon concept
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd video game logic, decent economy

jokes.text: This is that video game Reverend Lovejoy said you shouldn't play.
jokes.setup: Marge noting moral objection to the game
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to Lovejoy playing it himself
jokes.explanation: Classic hypocrisy reveal with good timing and structure

jokes.text: Well, he's playing it right now. Slay the wounded!
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy enthusiastically playing the violent game
jokes.explanation: Perfect character hypocrisy moment with violent gaming enthusiasm

jokes.text: My non-lazy eye!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy getting hit by something from the game
jokes.punchline: Referencing his lazy eye condition while being injured
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to character's established condition, good economy

jokes.text: My other eye!
jokes.setup: Following up the first eye injury
jokes.punchline: Now both eyes are injured, completing the pair
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and economy, but predictable follow-up

jokes.text: So which of these 'board lames' shall we play?
jokes.setup: Switching to board games after video game violence
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive portmanteau of 'board games' and 'lame'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Bart's attitude, good character voice

jokes.text: Taffy Land? Drops and Risers? Consternation? Ravenous, Ravenous Rhinos? Mouse Catch? Battleboat? Funopoly? Crate of Apes? Yahtzu? Tiddlywonks?
jokes.punchline: Parody names of real board games with slight variations
jokes.explanation: Clever parody names, though the list runs a bit long

jokes.text: Satan's Path?
jokes.punchline: The game is literally named after Satan
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the supernatural plot, clear warning ignored

jokes.text: Hey, it's got to be good if Satan put his name on it.
jokes.setup: Discovering Satan's involvement in the game
jokes.punchline: Bart's twisted endorsement logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - inversely valuing danger, good character consistency

jokes.text: Oh, I'm always the thimble.
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's resigned acceptance of the least cool piece
jokes.explanation: Classic Milhouse victim mentality, good character moment

jokes.text: There was a bank error in my favor. And I'm spending it all on Oriental Avenue prostitutes!
jokes.setup: Homer getting a Monopoly card benefit
jokes.punchline: Immediately using it for the most inappropriate purchase possible
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inappropriate behavior, subverts wholesome board game trope

jokes.text: I don't like the looks of that knight, Chief. Don't worry. We're safe. He's two steps away, and one to the right.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum confidently explaining knight move mechanics incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence, good chess knowledge joke

jokes.text: For me, it's game, set... and match.
jokes.setup: Character appearing to make tennis reference
jokes.punchline: Wrong sport reference in chess context
jokes.explanation: Mild sport confusion joke, not particularly clever

jokes.text: I think you mean 'check, and mate.' Just got crushed by a giant horse, Lou. You want to cut me a break?
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's injured irritation at being corrected
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics, Wiggum's wounded pride is funny

jokes.text: Oh, man, I'm never coming down! Ah! Whoo-hoo! Another ladder! Riding high! Sinking low! Top of the heap! Oh! Down I go! On top for good! Oh, cruel hubris!
jokes.setup: Barney playing Chutes and Ladders in real life
jokes.punchline: His emotional roller coaster matching the game mechanics
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy concept, perfect for Barney's dramatic personality

jokes.text: Yatszu. Here we go, back in the cup. At least the cup is lined with felt. Hey, Mr. Positive, shut the hell up.
jokes.setup: Ralph being rolled as human dice
jokes.punchline: His optimistic observation and the dice's rude response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph optimism even in absurd circumstances, good contrast

jokes.text: B-7. They dunked our Battleboat!
jokes.punchline: Using 'dunked' instead of 'sunk' for the basketball parody game
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on basketball/battleship mashup

jokes.text: I wish I could see my mom one last time, so I can say, 'This is all your fault!'
jokes.setup: Milhouse facing death and wanting to see his mother
jokes.punchline: Not for love, but to blame her for his situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - subverts deathbed sentiment with classic Milhouse resentment

jokes.text: Colonel Ketchup, I say it was you that killed him in the parlor, with the letter-opener!
jokes.punchline: Marge playing detective with condiment-themed character
jokes.explanation: Standard Clue parody, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: No. I might sink the letter. And 'Q' is just too valuable. That blank could be any letter we want. Maybe I'm just tired of living.
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing Scrabble strategy over life, then existential despair
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from game obsession to suicidal ideation

jokes.text: The final challenge: Mousecatch. A game so lame, no one's ever finished it.
jokes.punchline: The game is too boring for anyone to complete
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about board game tedium

jokes.text: Crazy Eights! Think, Bart. Think! What was that lesson I learned from video games? Oh, yeah! Kill, kill, kill!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to remember an important lesson
jokes.punchline: The lesson is just violence from video games
jokes.explanation: Good Bart character moment, calls back to video game violence theme

jokes.text: There comes a time to beat the crap out of childish things.
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-mature philosophy about destroying toys
jokes.explanation: Decent mock-wisdom from Bart, good character voice

jokes.text: Sure is a hard way to catch a mouse.
jokes.setup: After the elaborate Mousetrap game sequence
jokes.punchline: Understated observation about the complexity
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan comment on the absurdity of Mousetrap

jokes.text: Oh, this stupid thing never works.
jokes.setup: The Mousetrap game failing to work
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about the actual game's notorious unreliability
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about real Mousetrap game mechanics, relatable

jokes.text: That's it. From now on, we'll just play Hangman. Wait, wait. We still got one more letter. Is it... 3?
jokes.punchline: Homer guessing a number instead of a letter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity moment, good game knowledge subversion

jokes.text: That's not a letter. Stupid, stupid, stupid.
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-flagellation over the obvious error
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character beat, familiar self-criticism pattern

jokes.text: Oh, Homie, what a great idea-- to take a cruise in uncharted waters. Yeah, charts are for squares, baby.
jokes.punchline: Homer's dangerously casual attitude toward navigation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for maritime disaster, classic Homer recklessness

jokes.text: Lenny, I'm on a second honeymoon! You'll have to pick your own Lotto numbers.
jokes.punchline: Still managing mundane workplace lottery duties
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Homer's work relationships

jokes.text: Oh, baby, let's give those seagulls something to squawk about.
jokes.punchline: Crude euphemism involving seabirds
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer attempted romance, mildly crude but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Oh yeah, baby, I like it when you're distracted.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing romance while Marge is clearly focused elsewhere
jokes.punchline: Homer's obliviousness to Marge's distraction
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness, though not particularly clever

jokes.text: This poor man must have been out there for days. He's nothing but hard, bronze muscle.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing sympathy for shipwreck survivor
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently sexualizing his physical condition
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo from innocent Marge, sets up her attraction

jokes.text: So... uh, you know... I'm not really in the mood anymore. Fine. I'm going for a walk.
jokes.punchline: Awkward sexual tension deflation
jokes.explanation: Realistic awkwardness, but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Unfortunately, he had the strength of ten businessmen.
jokes.punchline: Measuring strength in units of businessmen
jokes.explanation: Clever absurd measurement system, good economy and originality

jokes.text: Hey. I've had a hard trip, too. One of the lenses fell out of my sunglasses!
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing trivial sunglasses problem to near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness and lack of perspective, great contrast

jokes.text: Why do piemakers get all the girls?
jokes.punchline: Homer's jealous misunderstanding of attraction
jokes.explanation: Good Homer jealousy moment, relatable male insecurity

jokes.text: It was poisoned! No it wasn't! Well, it was rhubarb. No, it was berry-peach. Berry-peach? That's my favorite!
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately wanting the 'dangerous' pie when it's her favorite flavor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's priorities, nice escalation

jokes.text: I guess we've got to kill him before he kills us. Well, we'd better do it quick, because he's making scones!
jokes.setup: Deciding to kill Roger in self-defense
jokes.punchline: Urgency based on his continued baking rather than danger
jokes.explanation: Clever priority inversion - more worried about food temptation than murder

jokes.text: They should call this one 'Recipe For Murder.' What do you mean, 'this one'?
jokes.setup: Homer making a pun about their situation
jokes.punchline: Marge's confusion implying this has happened before
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and suggestion of recurring murder situations

jokes.text: Roger was telling the truth. This proves nothing. Roger could've been the one that killed them. Soy sauce. Low sodium. Then his story was true.
jokes.punchline: Homer's stubborn denial followed by immediate acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good character stubbornness followed by quick reversal

jokes.text: The pie you baked us killed that shark! The pie was clean. The shark died from the fuel leaking out of your boat.
jokes.setup: Blaming Roger's pie for the shark's death
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was Homer's boat causing environmental damage
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and environmental humor, though not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, goody. The monkey got ahold of a spear gun.
jokes.setup: Already tense situation with attempted murder
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about dangerous animal with weapon
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and deadpan delivery of absurd danger

jokes.text: Why did you do that?! We had to kill him or he'd tell people we tried to kill him.
jokes.punchline: Homer's circular logic about covering up attempted murder
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark logic - escalating crime to cover previous crime

jokes.text: You're alive? Yes. I suspected our host would poison us, so I gave everyone an antidote before dinner.
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was prepared for poisoning all along
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist but feels a bit convenient

jokes.text: She just wanted to ride bikes through New England. But those seats hurt my ass.
jokes.punchline: Reducing her dreams to his own physical discomfort
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer selfishness even in grief, perfect character moment

jokes.text: Ever wonder what she thinks about? Just sugarplums and buttercups.
jokes.punchline: Assumption about innocent thoughts
jokes.explanation: Sets up contrast for upcoming vampire segment, but weak on its own

jokes.text: Hey, what's that weird look on all those girls' faces? It's something you've never seen: they're smitten.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dig about Bart never inspiring romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic and Bart's romantic inexperience

jokes.text: I love your pallor. Why is there a steering wheel in my bedroom?
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical concern about bus steering wheel
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between romance and practical bus crash concerns

jokes.text: How'd you do that? Oh, it's these cheap school buses, with their squishy metal.
jokes.punchline: Edmund's casual explanation of bus construction quality
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about school bus quality

jokes.text: On another subject, you have beautiful eyes. They're just dots in circles.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's literal description of cartoon eyes
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about cartoon animation style

jokes.text: You're a vampire. I should be scared, but I'm not. Let us move between the trees the way a bat does: by jumping.
jokes.punchline: Edmund's incorrect understanding of bat movement
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about vampire/bat misconceptions

jokes.text: Lisa's fallen for a vampire? That makes me so angry! I feel the change coming!
jokes.punchline: His anger triggering werewolf transformation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation connecting paternal anger to supernatural transformation

jokes.text: I know, I know. Don't serve garlic, don't stab your guest in the heart with a wooden stake, don't ask him if he knows Frankenstein. It's racist somehow.
jokes.punchline: Treating monster stereotypes as racism
jokes.explanation: Clever modern sensitivity applied to classic monster tropes

jokes.text: Dad, I don't need a chaperone. I'm 400 years old. You live in my crypt, you play by my rules.
jokes.punchline: Dracula using 'my house, my rules' despite Edmund's age
jokes.explanation: Good parental authority joke with vampire twist

jokes.text: Okay, okay. If a mosquito bites you, does it become a vampire, too? Yes.
jokes.punchline: Simple confirmation of absurd vampire mosquito concept
jokes.explanation: Decent vampire logic exploration, but not exceptional

jokes.text: Okay, okay. If you bite your tongue, does it become a vampire? Look, I'm more than just a vampire. I'm a nut for Dixieland jazz.
jokes.punchline: Edmund deflecting to his jazz hobby
jokes.explanation: Mild character development through unexpected hobby reveal

jokes.text: You said you weren't going to bring that. I said I might not.
jokes.setup: Lisa objecting to Edmund bringing his instrument
jokes.punchline: Edmund's technicality about his promise
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship argument about technicalities

jokes.text: They say vampires live forever, but I die of embarrassment every day.
jokes.punchline: Dying metaphorically from social embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on death/immortality with relatable embarrassment

jokes.text: Keep your mouth closed, or you'll swallow a lot of bugs.
jokes.punchline: Practical flying advice about insects
jokes.explanation: Good practical consideration for fantasy flying

jokes.text: Man, you vamps got it made. You sleep all day... And, because we only drive at night, we can really time the traffic lights. Watch. Green. Green. Green. Boom. Green. Yes. Boom. Green. Good. Boom.
jokes.punchline: Mundane traffic light timing as major vampire advantage
jokes.explanation: Clever focus on mundane benefits of supernatural lifestyle

jokes.text: Excuse me. Have you seen these kids? I did. And they were sneaking kisses. One kiss! Ha, ha, ha. Two kisses! Ha, ha, ha. Three kisses!
jokes.setup: Homer searching for Lisa and Edmund
jokes.punchline: Vampire's gleeful counting of romantic progression
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and vampire character voice

jokes.text: I'm Dracula, not The Hulk.
jokes.setup: Someone expecting Dracula to have superhuman strength
jokes.punchline: Dracula clarifying his specific supernatural abilities
jokes.explanation: Good character clarification about monster power differences

jokes.text: Eight forever? Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa. I can't spend eternity using kid scissors. They barely cut anything.
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about inferior children's tools
jokes.explanation: Excellent practical consideration about eternal childhood, very Lisa-like

jokes.text: Pretty ironic-- a cross being used to kill someone.
jokes.setup: Cross being used as weapon against vampire
jokes.punchline: Lisa noting the irony of Christian symbol causing death
jokes.explanation: Sharp intellectual observation, perfect for Lisa's character

jokes.text: Whoa, you blood-sucking freaks love your kids, too? I remember when he was a baby, I'd sneak up to his crib in the middle of the day to make sure he was still not breathing.
jokes.setup: Homer surprised by vampire parental love
jokes.punchline: Dracula's twisted version of checking on sleeping baby
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor inverting normal parental behavior for vampires

jokes.text: Well, nobody loves his kids more than me! Come and get it! Die, fatso!
jokes.punchline: Immediately offering himself as vampire food
jokes.explanation: Good Homer reckless bravery to prove his love

jokes.text: His blood-- it's full of cholesterol. And not the good kind.
jokes.punchline: Complaining about Homer's unhealthy blood quality
jokes.explanation: Clever health-conscious vampire and Homer's poor diet

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! Uh, Dad? They turned you into a vampire. Yes! I can fly! See you back at the house.
jokes.setup: Lisa informing Homer he's been turned
jokes.punchline: Homer's excitement about vampire powers instead of concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - focusing on fun benefits over consequences

tmdb_overview: Worried about the effects of violent video games, Marge encourages Bart and Milhouse to play board games; Lisa becomes smitten with a new student, a vampire; while on a second honeymoon, Homer and Marge rescue a castaway who escaped from his ship.
